title: Replace tipper end stop assembly
summary: Replace a damaged tipper end stop by locking out the tipper, accessing the end stop as needed, removing the damaged end stop, installing a new end stop with two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite®, and checking the opposite paired end stop for damage.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tipper end stop is damaged and requires replacement. The source also indicates the opposite end stop should be checked because end stops typically work in pairs.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a diagnosis-only workflow when no damaged end stop has been identified.
Do not assume paired collision damage applies without inspection when belt failure is the cause, because the source notes belt failure as an exception.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform LOTO, remove access panel if needed, replace the end stop hardware, apply Loctite®, and inspect the opposite end stop for damage.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before performing this procedure.
Use new M8x40 socket-head cap screws when installing the replacement end stop.
access_or_tools_needed: Two M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop

system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out before replacement work begins.
Replacement end stop and new mounting hardware are available before installation.

external_references.description: End Stop Assembly Replacement source section.
Replacement subsection for end stop assembly.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-12 End Stop maintenance diagram showing the end stop location and replacement context.

steps.title: Lock out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before accessing and replacing the end stop.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the source before starting any removal or installation work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied and the tipper is in a maintenance-safe state.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out or safe isolation cannot be confirmed.
stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if LOTO cannot be completed.
Escalate if safe isolation of the tipper cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the source.

steps.title: Gather replacement parts, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before disassembly begins.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement end stop, two M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, a 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.
steps.expected_result: All required parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement end stop, new screws, PPE, and required hand tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Any required replacement part, PPE item, or tool is missing.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the required replacement parts, PPE, and tools: end stop, two M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the replacement end stop is not available.
Stop if new M8x40 socket-head cap screws are not available.
Stop if required PPE or tools are missing.

source_evidence.text: Gather the required replacement parts, PPE, and tools: end stop, two M8x40 socket-head cap screws per end stop, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 6-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.
The procedure calls for removal of two M8 socket-head screws and installation of a new end stop with two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the end stop mounting area when the side panel blocks access.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the area.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and the end stop area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The end stop mounting area can be reached without obstruction.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed or access to the end stop remains blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: End stop location and surrounding mounting area for access orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the side panel cannot be removed safely.
Escalate if access to the end stop area is still not possible after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the area.
The procedure calls for possible side panel removal.

steps.title: Remove the damaged end stop
steps.purpose: Detach the damaged end stop from its mounting location so it can be replaced.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.
steps.expected_result: The damaged end stop is removed from the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The end stop and its two mounting screws are removed without damaging the mounting area.
steps.failure_condition: The end stop cannot be removed or the mounting area is damaged during removal.

actions.label: Remove end stop screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The end stop and the two M8x40 socket-head screw mounting points.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the end stop mounting screws cannot be removed.
Escalate if the mounting area appears damaged beyond end stop replacement.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two M8 socket-head screws holding the end stop in place.
The procedure calls for removal of two M8 socket-head screws.

steps.title: Install the new end stop
steps.purpose: Restore the end stop assembly using the specified replacement hardware and threadlocker.
steps.instruction: Mount the new end stop using two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.
steps.expected_result: The new end stop is installed and secured with new hardware and Loctite®.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement end stop is mounted in place with two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite®.
steps.failure_condition: The new end stop cannot be secured correctly or required new hardware is not used.

actions.label: Install new mounting screws with Loctite
actions.exact_action: Use two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Replacement end stop position and mounting orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if new M8x40 socket-head cap screws are not available.
Stop if Loctite® is not available.
Escalate if the replacement end stop does not fit or cannot be mounted securely.

source_evidence.text: Mount the new end stop using two new M8 socket-head screws and Loctite®.
The procedure calls for installation of a new end stop with two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite.

steps.title: Inspect the opposite end stop and replace if needed
steps.purpose: Check for paired damage because the source states end stops typically work in pairs.
steps.instruction: Check the opposite end stop for damage and replace it if necessary. Note that end stops typically work in pairs, and a collision at the bottom often suggests one of the top end stops is also damaged. Belt failure is noted by the source as an exception.
steps.expected_result: The opposite end stop has been inspected and replaced if damage is found.
steps.healthy_condition: The opposite end stop shows no damage, or it has also been replaced if damage was present.
steps.failure_condition: The opposite end stop is damaged and left unaddressed, or the damage pattern cannot be explained.

actions.label: Inspect opposite end stop
actions.exact_action: Check the opposite end stop for damage.

actions.label: Replace opposite end stop if needed

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: End stop identification and comparison points for checking the opposite paired end stop.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the opposite end stop is also damaged.
Escalate if the observed damage pattern does not match expected paired collision behavior and belt failure is not evident.

source_evidence.text: Check the opposite end stop for damage and replace it if necessary, noting the source statement that end stops typically work in pairs and bottom collision damage often suggests corresponding top end stop damage.
The source notes that end stops typically work in pairs; damage at the bottom often suggests one of the top end stops is also damaged.
An exception noted by the source is a belt failure, which may explain damage without the usual paired collision pattern.

The end stops typically work in pairs. A collision at the bottom often means there is also a collision at the top, so a damaged bottom end stop may indicate one of the top end stops is also damaged, with belt failure noted as an exception.

success_criteria: The damaged end stop is replaced.
The new end stop is secured using two new M8x40 socket-head cap screws and Loctite®.
The opposite paired end stop has been checked for damage.
If the opposite end stop was damaged, it has also been replaced.
healthy_conditions: Tipper was locked out before maintenance work began.
Required PPE, tools, and replacement parts were available.
The end stop mounting area was accessible.
The replacement end stop is installed securely with new hardware and Loctite®.
The opposite end stop shows no damage or has been replaced if damaged.
failure_conditions: LOTO cannot be completed or confirmed.
Required replacement parts, PPE, or tools are missing.
The side panel cannot be removed when access is needed.
The damaged end stop cannot be removed.
The replacement end stop cannot be installed securely.
The opposite end stop is damaged and not addressed.
Observed damage pattern is inconsistent and cannot be explained by the source-noted belt failure exception.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if LOTO cannot be completed or safe isolation cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if the end stop mounting area is damaged beyond simple end stop replacement.
Escalate if the opposite end stop is also damaged.
Escalate if the damage pattern is unclear and belt failure does not explain the condition.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Figure 7-12 End Stop for end stop location, mounting area, and paired end stop inspection context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-12 appears on page 155 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_12_1_replacement.
